examples.title: Randomized Quicksort Pseudocode
examples.description: Standard pseudocode showing random pivot selection and partition
examples.code:
RANDOMIZED-QUICKSORT(A, p, r)
  if p < r
    q = RANDOMIZED-PARTITION(A, p, r)
    RANDOMIZED-QUICKSORT(A, p, q-1)
    RANDOMIZED-QUICKSORT(A, q+1, r)

RANDOMIZED-PARTITION(A, p, r)
  i = RANDOM(p, r)          // Generate random index
  swap A[r] with A[i]       // Move random pivot to end
  return PARTITION(A, p, r) // Use standard partition

PARTITION(A, p, r)
  x = A[r]                   // Pivot is last element
  i = p - 1
  for j = p to r-1
    if A[j] <= x
      i = i + 1
      swap A[i] with A[j]
  swap A[i+1] with A[r]
  return i + 1
examples.explanation: Randomized-Partition first selects a random element and swaps it to the end, then calls standard Partition. This ensures random pivot selection while using simple partition logic.
examples.timeComplexity: Average O(n log n), Worst O(n²)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(log n) average, O(n) worst for recursion stack

examples.title: Randomized Quicksort in Python
examples.description: Complete Python implementation with random pivot selection
examples.code:
import random

def randomized_quicksort(arr, low, high):
    if low < high:
        pi = randomized_partition(arr, low, high)
        randomized_quicksort(arr, low, pi - 1)
        randomized_quicksort(arr, pi + 1, high)

def randomized_partition(arr, low, high):
    # Random pivot selection
    rand_idx = random.randint(low, high)
    arr[high], arr[rand_idx] = arr[rand_idx], arr[high]
    
    # Partition using Lomuto scheme
    pivot = arr[high]
    i = low - 1
    
    for j in range(low, high):
        if arr[j] <= pivot:
            i += 1
            arr[i], arr[j] = arr[j], arr[i]
    
    arr[i + 1], arr[high] = arr[high], arr[i + 1]
    return i + 1

# Example usage:
arr = [10, 7, 8, 9, 1, 5]
n = len(arr)
randomized_quicksort(arr, 0, n - 1)
print("Sorted array:", arr)
examples.explanation: Python implementation uses random.randint() to select pivot index, swaps it to end, then performs Lomuto partition. In-place sorting with average O(n log n) performance.
examples.timeComplexity: Average O(n log n), Worst O(n²)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(log n) average, O(n) worst for recursion

examples.title: 3-Way Partition Quicksort (Dutch National Flag)
examples.code:
def quicksort_3way(arr, low, high):
    if low >= high:
        return
    
    # 3-way partition: < pivot, = pivot, > pivot
    pivot = arr[low]
    lt = low      // arr[low..lt-1] < pivot
    gt = high     // arr[gt+1..high] > pivot
    i = low       // arr[lt..i-1] = pivot
    
    while i <= gt:
        if arr[i] < pivot:
            arr[lt], arr[i] = arr[i], arr[lt]
            lt += 1
            i += 1
        elif arr[i] > pivot:
            arr[gt], arr[i] = arr[i], arr[gt]
            gt -= 1
        else:
            i += 1
    
    # Recursively sort left and right parts
    quicksort_3way(arr, low, lt - 1)
    quicksort_3way(arr, gt + 1, high)

# Example:
arr = [4, 2, 4, 6, 4, 1, 4, 8]
quicksort_3way(arr, 0, len(arr) - 1)
print(arr)  # Output: [1, 2, 4, 4, 4, 4, 6, 8]
examples.explanation: 3-way partition solves the duplicate element problem by dividing array into three parts. Best case O(n) when all elements are equal. Maintains invariants with three pointers.
examples.timeComplexity: Average O(n log n), Best O(n) with all duplicates, Worst O(n²)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(log n) average, O(n) worst for recursion stack

examples.title: Iterative Randomized Quicksort with Stack
examples.code:
def iterative_quicksort(arr):
    stack = []
    stack.append((0, len(arr) - 1))
    
    while stack:
        low, high = stack.pop()
        
        if low >= high:
            continue
        
        # Random pivot selection
        rand_idx = random.randint(low, high)
        arr[high], arr[rand_idx] = arr[rand_idx], arr[high]
        
        # Partition
        pivot = arr[high]
        i = low - 1
        
        for j in range(low, high):
            if arr[j] <= pivot:
                i += 1
                arr[i], arr[j] = arr[j], arr[i]
        
        arr[i + 1], arr[high] = arr[high], arr[i + 1]
        pi = i + 1
        
        # Push larger subarray first (for O(log n) stack)
        left_size = pi - 1 - low
        right_size = high - (pi + 1)
        
        if left_size > right_size:
            stack.append((low, pi - 1))
            stack.append((pi + 1, high))
        else:
            stack.append((pi + 1, high))
            stack.append((low, pi - 1))

arr = [3, 6, 8, 10, 1, 2, 1]
iterative_quicksort(arr)
print(arr)
examples.explanation: Iterative version uses explicit stack instead of recursion. Pushing larger subarray first ensures O(log n) stack depth. Eliminates risk of stack overflow for large arrays.
examples.timeComplexity: Average O(n log n), Worst O(n²)
